title: Helper scripts for claude invocation
description: Shell scripts for common usage patterns: claude.sh wraps ccwrapper with default flags, agent-prompt.sh pipes a prompt file into claude via ccwrapper, clean-claude.sh resets Claude state by clearing config/cache directories.

title: Update README.md to reflect Plan/Refine/Execute workflow
description: The README.md is outdated. It describes ccwrapper as an ad-hoc chat tool with context accumulation. The project has been redesigned around a Plan → Refine → Execute workflow. The README needs a complete rewrite covering: (1) the new workflow model — Plan breaks work into epics via br, Refine asks clarifying questions, Execute picks up and implements epics; (2) the current tab layout (Plan, Output, Refine, Execute, System, Command, State); (3) prompt files in prompts/ (plan.md, refine.md, execute.md); (4) br/beads integration for issue tracking; (5) updated CLI flags; (6) removal of context accumulation and ad-hoc chat. This is a single-epic task: rewrite README.md to accurately describe the current project.

title: Answer Question → Refine inline editing feature
description: When the user clicks 'Answer Question' on a collapsed turn in the Output tab, it should: (1) extract the latest assistant text reply from that turn, (2) switch to the Refine tab, (3) populate a new editable textarea in the Refine panel with that text so the user can edit/answer questions inline, and (4) show a 'Send Answers' button that sends the edited content as the next prompt to Claude. This epic covers all UI and backend changes needed.
close_reason: All sub-tasks completed: Answer Question button and Refine inline editor fully implemented

title: CLI flag passthrough to claude
description: Passes all CLI arguments through to the underlying claude command. Supports --allowedTools, --model, --permission-mode, --allow-dangerously-skip-permissions, and any other claude flags. Also respects CLAUDE_CODE_SIMPLE env var to set --output-format=stream-json.

title: Remove Refresh buttons from HTML and JS
description:
Remove the Refresh buttons from the Refine and Execute tabs in the web UI.

## Files to edit
- cmd/ccwrapper/web/index.html
- cmd/ccwrapper/web/app.js

## Changes required

### index.html
1. In the refine-panel (line ~310): Remove the `<button id="btn-refresh-br-refine" ...>Refresh</button>` element
2. In the execute-panel (line ~326): Remove the `<button id="btn-refresh-br" ...>Refresh</button>` element

### app.js
1. Remove the `const btnRefreshBr = document.getElementById('btn-refresh-br');` declaration (line ~16)
2. Remove the `const btnRefreshBrRefine = document.getElementById('btn-refresh-br-refine');` declaration (line ~20)
3. Remove `btnRefreshBr.addEventListener('click', loadBrList);` (line ~103)
4. Remove `btnRefreshBrRefine.addEventListener('click', loadBrListRefine);` (line ~132)

## Notes
- Keep the automatic loading on tab switch (loadBrList/loadBrListRefine called in tab click handler) — only remove the manual Refresh buttons
- Keep the loadBrList() and loadBrListRefine() functions themselves — they are still used
- After changes, run `make build` to verify the build succeeds
close_reason: Removed Refresh buttons from both Refine and Execute tabs in index.html and app.js

title: Add .btn-answer CSS styles back to index.html
description: The .btn-answer and .btn-answer:hover CSS rules were removed from index.html during the refactor. Restore these styles so the Answer Question button renders correctly in turn summaries. The original styles were: .btn-answer { font-size: 0.75rem; padding: 0.2rem 0.6rem; margin-left: 0.5rem; opacity: 0.6; cursor: pointer; vertical-align: middle; } .btn-answer:hover { opacity: 1; } Files to edit: cmd/ccwrapper/web/index.html
close_reason: Superseded by new Answer Question feature design that uses Refine tab with inline editing

title: Remove Context tab from web UI
description: Project does not carry context between claude runs, so the Context tab and all context accumulation logic should be removed.
close_reason: Removed Context tab, context file, /api/context, /api/clear endpoints, HandleContext, HandleClear, BuildContextEntry, and context accumulation. Prompt is now passed directly to claude.

title: Container support for sandboxed Claude sessions
description: Containerfile based on Ubuntu with Node.js, Go, and common dev tools. Allows running ccwrapper+claude in an isolated container environment. Port 7080 exposed for web dashboard access.

title: Restore Answer Question button in Output tab
description: The 'Answer Question' button was removed during the Plan/Execute/Refine tab refactor. This button appeared in the turn summary when a turn collapsed, letting the user respond to questions from the assistant. Without it, users cannot respond to questions raised during Refine runs. The button needs to be restored and adapted to work with the new tab-based UI (e.g., populate the Plan editor and switch to the Plan tab).
close_reason: Superseded by new Answer Question feature design

title: Add Answer Question button back to turn summaries in app.js
description: When a turn is collapsed (in collapseTurn()), the old code called addAnswerButton(turn) which: 1) gathered all .block-text content from the turn, 2) created a button with class 'btn-answer' and text 'Answer Question', 3) appended it to the turn's .turn-summary element, 4) on click, called enterAnswerMode() with the gathered text. Restore this addAnswerButton() function in app.js. Instead of the old enterAnswerMode() (which populated a now-removed header prompt input), the button click should: populate the Plan editor textarea with the assistant's text, switch to the Plan tab, and focus the editor. Files to edit: cmd/ccwrapper/web/app.js
close_reason: Superseded by new Answer Question feature design that uses Refine tab with inline editing

title: ANSI/VT escape sequence parser
description: Real-time parser for Claude Code's terminal output. Strips ANSI escape sequences, extracts structured data including model name, token counts (in/out/cache), estimated cost, and active tool calls. Handles Claude's status-line protocol and multi-line escape sequences.

title: Web dashboard with SSE streaming
description: Single-page web UI served on localhost:7080. Connects via Server-Sent Events to stream parsed Claude session data in real-time. Vanilla HTML/JS, no build step. Tabs: Overview (model, tokens, cost, running totals), Tools (active tool calls with collapsible args), State (Claude config file inspector).

title: Makefile build system
description: Simple Makefile with build (darwin/arm64 via CGO), install (copies to /usr/local/bin), and clean targets. Produces a single static binary.

title: Running token and cost totals across sessions
description: Tracks cumulative input/output/cache tokens and estimated cost across the entire ccwrapper session, persisting totals even as Claude's internal counters reset between conversations. Displayed in the tab bar of the web UI.

title: State tab for inspecting Claude config paths
description: Web UI tab that reads and displays contents of ~/.claude/, ~/.claude.json, and ~/.cache/ directories. Lets users inspect Claude Code's configuration and cached state without leaving the dashboard.

title: PTY harness for Claude Code CLI
description: Go-based PTY wrapper that spawns claude CLI as a child process, captures raw terminal output via a pseudoterminal, and forwards it to both the real terminal and a web UI via SSE. Handles signal forwarding (SIGINT, SIGWINCH) and clean shutdown.

title: Add inline text editor and Send Answers button to Refine panel
description: Redesign the Refine panel to support inline answer editing. Add a <textarea> element (id='refine-editor') to the Refine panel, styled like the plan-editor (monospace, flex:1, full height). This textarea is hidden by default and shown when the user clicks 'Answer Question'. Add a 'Send Answers' button (id='btn-send-answers') to the refine toolbar, also hidden by default. When 'Answer Question' is clicked: (1) the textarea is populated with the assistant's last reply text, (2) the textarea and Send Answers button become visible, (3) the existing refine-preview and br-list sections can remain visible below. When 'Send Answers' is clicked: (1) take the textarea content as the prompt, (2) send it via POST /api/prompt, (3) switch to Output tab and start a new turn. The existing 'Send Refine' button should remain and work independently. Files to edit: cmd/ccwrapper/web/index.html (new textarea + button HTML), cmd/ccwrapper/web/app.js (answerQuestion function, send answers handler, DOM references).
close_reason: Added refine-editor textarea, btn-send-answers button, sendAnswers handler, and answerQuestion integration

title: Remove Refresh buttons from Refine and Execute tabs
description: Epic: Remove the Refresh buttons from the Refine and Execute tabs in the web UI. These buttons (btn-refresh-br-refine and btn-refresh-br) manually reload the br list, but they should be removed. This involves: (1) removing the button elements from index.html, (2) removing the JS references and click handlers in app.js. The br list data should still load automatically when the tabs are opened — only the manual Refresh buttons are removed.
close_reason: Epic completed: Refresh buttons removed from Refine and Execute tabs

title: Add Answer Question button to collapsed turns in Output tab
description: When a turn finishes and collapses, add an 'Answer Question' button to the turn-summary element. The button should: (1) gather all .block-text content from the turn's body, (2) store it for later use, (3) on click, call a function that navigates to the Refine tab and populates the editor. Add .btn-answer CSS styles to index.html (font-size 0.75rem, padding 0.2rem 0.6rem, margin-left 0.5rem, opacity 0.6 with hover 1.0). The button should only appear when the turn has text content from the assistant. Files to edit: cmd/ccwrapper/web/index.html (CSS), cmd/ccwrapper/web/app.js (addAnswerButton function called from collapseTurn, click handler calls answerQuestion(text)).
close_reason: Implemented addAnswerButton, answerQuestion functions and .btn-answer CSS
